user_query:
I’m setting up a small, local “CI dry run” for my math practice utilities and need your help to execute and report the results in a way I can share with my mentor.

Inputs are in the provided repo layout under input/. Please do not modify any files under input/.

Requirements:
1) Run tests and capture output
- Use Python 3 and run the test suite from the repository root with this exact command:
  python -m unittest discover -s input/tests -p 'test_*.py' -v
- Save the complete console output of that command to artifacts/test-results.txt (create the artifacts/ folder if needed).
- From the captured output, compute a summary with:
  - total number of tests run
  - number passed
  - number failed
  - the fully qualified names of failing tests and their first-line failure messages
  - the exact command used and an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when you ran it
- Save that summary as JSON at artifacts/test-summary.json with keys: total, passed, failed, failures (array of objects with test and message), command, timestamp.

2) Prepare meeting notes with actionable next steps
- Create docs/meeting_notes.md (create the docs/ folder if needed) with three sections:
  - Context: one short paragraph stating this is a local CI dry run for my math-utils practice and that I’m pursuing a math degree online.
  - Results Summary: include total, passed, failed, and list the fully qualified names of any failing tests from artifacts/test-summary.json.
  - Action Items: at least three bullet points. Include at least:
    - One item that explicitly mentions fixing the function implicated by the failing test(s) (e.g., ‘subtract’ if that’s what failed).
    - One item to add or refine edge-case tests informed by the failures.
    - One item to rerun the test command and update the artifacts after fixes.

3) Draft an email to my mentor summarizing the outcomes
- Create out/email_draft.txt (create the out/ folder if needed) as plain text with:
  - To: mentor@example.com
  - Subject: CI dry run results for math-utils
  - Body: a brief summary paragraph including the test counts (total/passed/failed), the names of any failing tests, and links to the local artifact paths (artifacts/test-results.txt and artifacts/test-summary.json). End with a short request for feedback on next steps.

Deliverables to produce:
- artifacts/test-results.txt (raw test output)
- artifacts/test-summary.json (structured summary)
- docs/meeting_notes.md (notes with action items)
- out/email_draft.txt (email draft)

Notes:
- Keep everything self-contained; do not use the internet or any external dependencies beyond the Python standard library.
- All outputs should be derived from the provided input files and the actual test run you perform.
- Ensure the numbers and failing test names in docs/meeting_notes.md and out/email_draft.txt match artifacts/test-summary.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
